text: The same reason people love Madonna, Beyoncé, Lady Gaga, or any other diva. Femininity is so often portrayed as being down-to-earth, sweet, gentle, mothering, girl-next-door. That’s the standard that most women are expected to meet. We need to be soft and small and submissive because men are strong and aggressive. And if a piece of media attempts to showcase a woman in a different way, they have them be more masculine. They shit on women and femininity and act like men because men are superior. It’s nice to see a presentation of femininity that is domineering and glamorous. I don’t think anyone who loves Regina does so because they think she’d be a good friend. But she’s represents a spectrum of femininity and girlhood that we don’t get to see often. She’s a diva and she gets what she wants and she’s unapologetic about it.

text: Honestly, I feel like Cadys mom is the worst character in the movie. Her parents just drop her into public school and hope she stays the sweet, innocent girl she was. They dont give her any guidance or advice whatsoever, not even a cliched "stay away from (insert group of teenagers they consider 'bad')". When Cady comes back from her first day and is clearly not happy, neither of them go to check on her and ask her whats wrong, how they can help, nothing. they just look at each other and continue their day. The second day, she gives them vague and confusing answers: do they ask for elaboration? NOPE! They arent involved in her life at all, which makes it preschool-level easy to lie to them. At the talent show, its very obvious Cady has changed and what kind of girls shes with, and judging from their reaction to their dance, they didnt approve. Do they sit her down and have a conversation about sexuality, friends, boys, anything? NOPE. They just let it go! They dont even ask her ab grades bc they dont even know shes failing Math until near the end of the movie. And clearly, they didnt bother to pick up a parenting book or ask for guidance on parenting a teen in the US from any friends/family, or even communicate with each other because the father has no clue what grounding means. So the hostility from the mother after everything happens is, imo, completely unjustified. Cady isnt a baby, and she had to take responsibility for her actions. But at the same time, she is still an impressionable minor that has no guidance from her parents into a completely new environment. She had to learn about it on her own. The father at least acknowledged it somewhat saying they mainstreamed her too soon. When the mother realizes her change, its when she doesnt care about the stupid pots under the cabinets— like, really girl?? You only realize she changed NOW?! Where have you been all year?? Anyways... thats the end of my rant. Cadys mom isnt exactly a mean girl, but she isnt a good mom and that contributed to Cadys transformation into a mean girl. TL;DR: Cadys mom sucks.
label: Condemnation

text: I would call Karen  mean long before I called her nice. Karen is too dim to say anything malicious without being prompted, but she’s also too dim to have any moral fortitude. She participates in the creation of the burn book, she laughs along with people when they are mean, she says rude things about Gretchen when she thinks she’s talking to Regina, she ditches Regina just because Gretchen tells her to. She’s not nice. This is just a really accurate (though extremely condensed and edited for the viewer’s comfort) way of showing how “mean cliques” work. Karen and Gretchen never need to be straight up mean, they just need to be dumb or servile enough to go along with what the actively mean girls are doing. Even Regina is rarely explicitly rude to anyone’s face. She wouldn’t be popular if she was. She also wouldn’t be intimidating if she didn’t have Karen and Gretchen (and Cady) backing her up. If she was a lone asshole, everyone would hate her. But her backhanded behavior backed up by “not mean” people like Karen sends a message, like “you’d better not fight back, everyone will laugh at you.”

text: Regina is a reflection of how she is raised to compete with others for everything in her life.   That's not healthy.  She treats her friends like shit because they are not friends, they are her enemies.   The old saying, keep your friends close but your enemies closer.   She's gotten to the top by stepping on people, but once at the top, she has to maintain that level constantly so there is no off day of thinking about what she really wants.   It's about maintaining status all the time.   That's constant stress.  There is not one minute for herself.  What Regina gets is that this is what life outside of high school can be like for her.   It's about maintaining that 'perfect' image and holding onto the status that has been hard fought for.   Sadly, she already has the wealth and connections through family to get what she needs to be successful.  She's still a kid but acts like she's much older.  She's been sexualized too young as well.   I don't think writers actually understand the psychology of the 'mean' girl.   I remember sitting in a Starbucks a few years ago and across from me were two women who had brought their daughters with them.  The daughters had to be below the age of 11, possibly 9-10 range.  I usually don't pay attention to other people, but it was the convo between the girls that struck me.  They were talking about dieting and how they look.  I listened to the vapid convo the moms were having about their daughters.  This is how someone like Regina is raised.
label: Mixed

text: i think the people who are commenting back to those saying "janis is the one who started all of it so why is she attacking cady" are right - the whole thing originally started as a small joke between janis and cady, to just prat about and mess with regina a LITTLE. i don't think at all that the whole thing is about janis wanting revenge on regina, she never states in the beginning "this girl ruined my life, will you help me ruin hers?" she DOES state something similar, but she never uses it as the reason for messing with her in the first place, it was just for laughs. janis never meant for it to go as far as it did - resulting in aaron breaking up with regina, their teacher being accused of drug dealing and basically just a load of shit happening. as janis says, she and regina know and ADMIT that they're mean - cady always plays the innocent girl, and she COULD have wanted it all to stop, she KNEW it was getting out of control but all the rep went to her head. she LIKED being the queen, and she wasn't about to lose it all even if people got hurt in the process. cady could have done something to stop it, she knew it was going too far, but chose not to. that's why janis is so pissed at her.
label: Defense
